---
title: "Experiments"
description: "PySDM experiments connecting numerical representation, seeded-particle properties, aerosol environments, and response surfaces"
lang: en
page-layout: article
sidebar: pysdm-en
---

This series begins with super-droplet representation, then moves through seeded-particle properties, background aerosol environments, and a κ–radius–timing response surface. Every note separates the question, baseline, changed conditions, diagnostics, limitations, and next hypothesis.

::: {.callout-note title="Relationship to PySDM-Seeding-Lab"}
These four experiments were run independently on the **laboratory server with Visual Studio Code**. They were not executed through the PySDM-Seeding-Lab app; they are documented as precursor studies that later informed the Lab's experiment and validation design.
:::

## Experiment sequence

```text
Experiment 1  numerical representation and ensemble stability
      ↓
Experiment 2  dry radius · κ · timing sensitivity and growth pathway
      ↓
Experiment 3  background aerosol · fixed-number/mass comparison
      ↓
Experiment 4  activation-strength · timing response surface
```

::: {.manual-post-list}
::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-06-30 · Jinu</div>

### [Experiment 1. How Much Does Super-Droplet Resolution Change the Result?](posts/experiments/2026-06-30-numerical-representation/index.qmd)

A 4 × 4 representation grid and 50-seed ensembles separate transient collision noise from persistent rain-water response.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-03 · Jinu</div>

### [Experiment 2. Sensitivity to Seeded-Particle Properties and Injection Timing](posts/experiments/2026-07-03-parameter-sensitivity/index.qmd)

Dry radius, κ, and injection timing are connected to vapour consumption, condensational growth, and rain-size mass.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-06 · Jinu</div>

### [Experiment 3. Warm Hygroscopic Seeding Across Background Aerosol Environments](posts/experiments/2026-07-06-realistic-warm-seeding/index.qmd)

Clean marine, polluted continental, and warm-orographic backgrounds are compared under fixed-number and fixed-mass injection.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-08 · Jinu</div>

### [Experiment 4. A κ–Radius–Timing Response Surface](posts/experiments/2026-07-08-efficiency-response-surface/index.qmd)

A dense 2,016-condition grid maps activation strength and injection timing into an empirical final rain-water response.
:::
:::
